entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Tropical maize (Zea mays L.) used in hybrid breeding studies

entities.name: HETEROTIC GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of populations used to maximize heterosis in hybrid breeding

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: The process of crossing genetically diverse parents to produce superior offspring

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The superior performance of hybrid offspring compared to their parents

entities.name: GENETIC DISTANCE
entities.description: A measure of genetic divergence between populations or individuals

entities.name: SIMPLE SEQUENCE REPEAT (SSR) MARKERS
entities.description: Molecular markers used to determine genetic distance and diversity

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species

entities.name: TROPICAL MAIZE POPULATIONS
entities.description: Groups of maize adapted to tropical environments

entities.name: PANMICTIC MIDPARENT HETEROSIS (PMPH)
entities.description: A measure of heterosis relative to the average of the parents

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: The genetic material of one or more organisms

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Measurable characteristics of a crop related to its agricultural performance

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Different locations or conditions where agronomic traits were evaluated

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Variants of a gene or marker detected in genotypes

entities.name: ANALYSIS OF MOLECULAR VARIANCE (AMOVA)
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze genetic variation within and between populations

entities.name: SQUARED MODIFIED ROGER'S DISTANCE (MRD)
entities.description: A specific method for calculating genetic distance based on markers

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain harvested per unit area
